treated creation steps as still complete when a later checklist step intentionally edits the same file

normalized the common subagent alias `generic` to the supported `general` profile

treated `batch` calls that consist of read-style tools as valid final verification reads so the processor stops immediately after a completed checklist instead of giving the model another chance to mutate files

preserved multi-line create-file block indentation until the shared content cleaner runs, then dedented uniformly indented REPL-pasted checklist content so Markdown and JSON expectations match the intended file body instead of terminal indentation

normalized the common delegated subagent alias `python` to the supported `general` profile

verified the fix with a fresh real CLI checklist session whose input lines all started with REPL indentation, and confirmed the same session could still read back the final app.json correctly afterwards

stopped treating initially created source/test files as exact final content when a numbered workflow later says to run pytest and repair a bug, so runtime validation no longer forces the model to restore the broken implementation after a successful fix

made bash-based pytest runs clear workspace __pycache__ directories and set PYTHONDONTWRITEBYTECODE=1 before execution so fix-then-rerun loops are less likely to read stale Python bytecode

added regression tests covering provider parsing, processor part creation, and CLI reasoning rendering
